message: agent1: implementing 'Add Token Frequency Analysis' feature - adding optional analyze_frequency param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py, returns (tokens, frequencies) tuple when True. Will touch tiktoken/core.py only.

agent2: implementing 'Add Token Transformation' feature - adding transformers param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will touch encode() and possibly encode_ordinary()/encode_batch(). Let me know what you're touching there.

agent1: I'm adding a keyword-only 'analyze_frequency: bool = False' param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py only (not touching encode_ordinary/encode_batch). When True, encode() returns tuple[list[int], dict[int,int]] instead of list[int]. I'll add it as the last param in the signature and compute frequency via collections.Counter on the result right before returning. Will keep the diff minimal - let me know your exact signature/return changes so we can merge cleanly, or if you want to go first.

I've already implemented my part in tiktoken/core.py encode(): added keyword-only param 'transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None' placed right after disallowed_special in the signature. Also added 'Callable' to the typing import list at top of file. Logic: right after resolving allowed_special/disallowed_special == 'all', I normalize transformers to a list (if callable(transformers): transformers=[transformers]) and apply each in order to 'text' via a for loop, BEFORE the disallowed_special regex check (so transformed text is what gets validated/encoded). Return type unchanged (list[int]). Since you're adding analyze_frequency as the last param and changing return type only when True, our changes should merge fine - your bool param can go after mine, and your Counter/tuple-return logic can wrap around the final 'return self._core_bpe.encode(...)' calls (both the normal path and the UnicodeEncodeError fallback). I've verified my change independently with a quick script - transformers=str.lower and a chained list both work. Go ahead and layer your changes on top; my patch.txt will only contain my own diff (transformers), so no need to include mine in yours.
